abstract: This article examines the structural and methodological framework of the Dictionary of Neologisms, a crowdsourcing project hosted by the Language Observatory of the University of Warsaw. By analyzing both its micro- and macro-structure, the author details the criteria for acquiring, selecting, and lexicographically describing new lexical units. The dictionary is contextualized within the broader landscape of traditional and community-driven neography, illustrating how it balances amateur contributions with professional linguistic oversight. Furthermore, the study explores the sociolinguistic factors driving the emergence of contemporary Polish vocabulary—particularly within the decentralized "network society"—while addressing the conceptual ambiguity and temporal challenges inherent in defining what truly constitutes a "neologism."
